entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Cajanus cajan, the crop studied for the effects of flower removal on seed yield

entities.name: HYDERABAD
entities.description: City in India where field experiments were conducted

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: Country where field experiments were conducted

entities.name: FLOWER REMOVAL
entities.description: The removal of all flowers and young pods for up to 5 weeks or from alternate nodes

entities.name: SEED YIELD
entities.description: The final production of seeds per plant

entities.name: FLOWERING PERIOD
entities.description: The duration of the flowering stage of the plant

entities.name: SENESCENCE
entities.description: The biological aging process of the plant

entities.name: NUMBER OF SEEDS PER POD
entities.description: The quantity of seeds contained within a single pod

entities.name: WEIGHT PER SEED
entities.description: The mass of an individual seed

entities.name: WINTER CROP
entities.description: Pigeonpeas grown during the winter season

entities.name: HOT WEATHER
entities.description: Unfavourable temperature conditions during the maturation of winter crops

entities.name: COMPENSATION
entities.description: The plant's ability to offset the loss of early flowers by setting pods from later flowers or other nodes

relationships.description: Flower removal had little effect on yield when sown at normal time but reduced yield in winter crops
Flower removal extended the flowering period
Flower removal had relatively little effect on the number of seeds per pod
Flower removal had relatively little effect on the weight per seed
Pigeonpeas were grown in field experiments at Hyderabad
Pigeonpeas were grown in field experiments in India
Flower removal in winter crops resulted in yield reductions
Hot weather during maturation contributed to yield reductions in winter crops
Plants compensated for the loss of flowers caused by removal
Compensation helped maintain seed yield despite flower removal
